<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] The HTML entry point being isolated from the JS module graph confirms the build pipeline correctly separates the HTML shell from the compiled viewer bundle.
[observation] [info] With only 3 files this zone is very small; if additional static assets (fonts, icons, manifest) are added they should land here to keep the shell concept coherent.
[observation] [info] Zero coupling is ideal for a static asset zone — no action needed, and this boundary should be preserved as the viewer grows.
[pattern] [info] Zero-coupling zone acts as an inviolable boundary between static asset delivery and the JS module graph. This boundary is structurally enforced (no importable exports) rather than by convention, making it uniquely stable relative to all other zones in the web package.
[suggestion] [info] Document in zone context that viewer-static-shell (index.html + logos) pairs with the viewer subtree inside web-dashboard-application to form the complete viewer shell. Without this cross-reference, the split is invisible to contributors exploring the zone map for the first time.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling are expected for static assets — this zone has no runtime JavaScript dependencies
- The HTML file serves as the SPA mount point; its isolation from JS modules is correct and confirms the bundler boundary is respected
- Light/dark mode logos being co-located with the HTML shell is a sensible organizational choice since they are referenced directly from markup
- Zero coupling is ideal for a static asset zone — no action needed, and this boundary should be preserved as the viewer grows.
- With only 3 files this zone is very small; if additional static assets (fonts, icons, manifest) are added they should land here to keep the shell concept coherent.
- The HTML entry point being isolated from the JS module graph confirms the build pipeline correctly separates the HTML shell from the compiled viewer bundle.
- The only zone in the entire web package with zero imports in both directions — functions as a hard boundary marker between the HTML/asset delivery layer and the JavaScript module graph. Unlike all other zones, it cannot be entangled by transitive dependency growth.
- Zero-coupling zone acts as an inviolable boundary between static asset delivery and the JS module graph. This boundary is structurally enforced (no importable exports) rather than by convention, making it uniquely stable relative to all other zones in the web package.
- The conceptual 'viewer shell' is split across two zones: the HTML entry point (viewer-static-shell) and the compiled JavaScript shell live in web-dashboard-application. A developer looking for 'the viewer shell' must know to check two structurally unrelated zones. This is an unavoidable consequence of the bundler boundary, but it is undocumented — a one-line note in the zone's context.md linking to the JS side would eliminate the discovery gap.
- Document in zone context that viewer-static-shell (index.html + logos) pairs with the viewer subtree inside web-dashboard-application to form the complete viewer shell. Without this cross-reference, the split is invisible to contributors exploring the zone map for the first time.

</insights>
